commit.message:
feat: Build improvements (#142)

Addresses two recent feedback items:
- zig pollutes the user's .cache/zig directory, which may grow large
- Selene manifests take too long to write

The first item is managed by providing zig with a cache dir, which
subsequently sets `ZIG_LOCAL_CACHE_DIR` in the subprocess environment.

The second is managed by providing a `complete_manifest` bool flag to
`build()`. When True, the manifest aims to be a complete picture of the
build stages, for use with sharing and diagnosis. When False (default),
less information is written to the manifest. At current this reduced
information is in the form of stripping input artifact or intermediate
artifacts in the manifest, keeping only the final binary.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: increase timeouts for long-running agent pipelines

- HTTP request timeout: 30s → 120s (2min)
- waitForTask polling: 60s → 300s (5min)
- CLI waitForTask: 30s → 180s (3min)

Fixes timeout kills on LLM calls, URL fetching, and multi-step
pipelines that legitimately take 45-90 seconds.

* feat: configurable timeouts via CLI flags, env vars, and constructor options

Call options:
  --timeout <ms|Ns|Nm>       HTTP request timeout (default: 120s)
  --poll-timeout <ms|Ns|Nm>  Polling timeout for result (default: 300s)
  --poll-interval <ms|Ns|Nm> Time between polls (default: 1s)

Env vars: AG2AG_REQUEST_TIMEOUT, AG2AG_POLL_TIMEOUT, AG2AG_POLL_INTERVAL

Formats: plain ms, 30s, 5m

Fixes hardcoded timeouts that killed long-running agent pipelines
(LLM calls, URL fetching, multi-step processing).

Replace contract factories with registration-based ContractRegistry (#126511)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR was created with the assistance of GitHub Copilot.

## Summary

Replace `IContractFactory<T>` and 26 named factory classes with a flat
registration API: `ContractRegistry.Register<T>(version, creator)`.

## Motivation

The existing factory pattern requires modifying multiple files to add
contract support for new runtimes (e.g., NativeAOT). Each contract has a
dedicated factory class with a hardcoded version switch. Adding a new
runtime means touching every factory.

## Changes

### New registration API
```csharp
// Any package registers its contracts the same way:
public static void Register(ContractRegistry registry)
{
    registry.Register<IThread>(1, static t => new Thread_1(t));
    registry.Register<IRuntimeTypeSystem>(1, static t => new RuntimeTypeSystem_1(t));
    // ...
}
```

### CachingContractRegistry
- Zero contract knowledge — pure `(Type, int) → creator` registry
- Takes `params Action<ContractRegistry>[]` for composable registration
- Built-in CoreCLR and external packages use the same `Register<T>()`
API

### Contract constructors
- Simplified to take only `Target target`
- Each reads its own globals internally (Object_1, GC_1, Thread_1, etc.)

### CoreCLRContracts.cs (new)
- Single file registering all 26 CoreCLR contract versions
- Same pattern external packages would use

### Deleted (26 files)
- All named factory classes (ThreadFactory, ExceptionFactory,
ObjectFactory, etc.)

### Enables
- External contract packages (NativeAOT, Mono) without modifying the
main cDAC reader
- `ContractDescriptorTarget.TryCreate(addr, ...,
[CoreCLRContracts.Register, NativeAOTContracts.Register], out target)`

## Testing
All 1406 existing cDAC tests pass.

commit.message:
feat(conv): add diverse ensemble with per-member stage configs

Add member_stages parameter to ConvNEFEnsemble.  When provided, each
ensemble member uses its own stage configuration (patch size, filter
count, strategy), enabling diverse ensembles where members specialize
at different spatial scales.  This increases ensemble diversity beyond
what different random seeds alone provide.

When member_stages is not provided, all members share the same stages
config (existing behaviour).  Length validation ensures member_stages
matches n_members.

Adds per-member stage support to _run_conv_config in the conv_cifar
benchmark suite.  Adds two diverse ensemble configs:
- 6 members with patch sizes 3/3/5/5/7/7 + gcn + std
- Same with per-patch contrast normalization

Two new tests:
- test_diverse_member_stages: verifies different patch sizes per member
- test_member_stages_length_mismatch: validates error on wrong count

commit.message:
feat(conv): auto-inject train_data for data-driven head encoders

When ConvNEFPipeline uses a data-driven encoder strategy (whitened,
class_contrast, local_pca), the subsample conv features are now
automatically passed as train_data in encoder_kwargs.  This means
the user can simply set encoder_strategy="whitened" on the pipeline
and the NEF head neurons adapt to the conv feature distribution
without manual data wiring.

The injection only occurs when train_data is not already provided
in encoder_kwargs, so explicit overrides are still possible.

Adds whitened encoder configs to the conv_cifar Colab suite:
- 64f p5 baseline + whitened
- 64f p5 + patchnorm + gcn + std + whitened (full combo)

Adds test_whitened_head_encoders to verify the auto-injection
works end-to-end in a pipeline.

commit.message:
feat(conv): add per-patch contrast normalization

Add normalize_patches option to ConvNEFStage.  When enabled, each
extracted patch is normalised to zero mean and unit L2 norm before
computing PCA or k-means filters.  This removes brightness variation
and focuses filter learning on texture and edge patterns, following
the preprocessing recommended by Coates & Ng (2012).

At inference time, the same per-patch normalization is applied via
_forward_normalized(), which unfolds the input into patches, normalises
each one, and computes inner products with the learned filters.  This
is more expensive than a single conv2d call but captures the correct
behaviour: each spatial position is normalised independently.

Implementation details:
- _normalize_patches_inplace(): static method for zero-mean, unit-norm
  normalisation with epsilon for stability
- _forward_normalized(): unfold → normalise → matmul path, handles
  both PCA (abs activation) and k-means (triangle activation) paths
- Per-patch normalization passes through stage config dicts to
  ConvNEFPipeline and ConvNEFEnsemble without any changes to those
  classes (stage kwargs are forwarded transparently)

Adds 4 patch-norm configs to the conv_cifar Colab suite:
- 64f p5 baseline with patchnorm
- 64f/128f p5 + patchnorm + gcn + std
- ×5 ensemble + patchnorm + gcn + std + hflip

Adds 4 new tests:
- Stage shape with normalization
- Non-negativity with abs activation
- Output differs from non-normalized
- Pipeline integration test

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(conv): add parallel multi-scale stage mode

Add parallel=True option to ConvNEFPipeline and ConvNEFEnsemble.
When enabled, all conv stages process the original input independently
and their feature maps are concatenated along the channel dimension
before SPP pooling.  This enables multi-scale feature extraction with
different patch sizes (e.g. 3×3 + 5×5 + 7×7) in a single pipeline.

When parallel stages produce different spatial sizes (due to different
patch sizes with pool_size=1), feature maps are aligned to the minimum
spatial dimensions using adaptive average pooling before concatenation.

Includes _apply_stages() method that handles both sequential (default)
and parallel execution modes.  Stage fitting in parallel mode fits
each stage independently on the original input rather than chaining.

Adds multi-scale configs to the conv_cifar Colab suite:
- 32×3 patch sizes (96 total channels)
- 64×3 patch sizes (192 total channels) with GCN+standardize

Two new tests: multi-scale shape verification and single-stage
parallel mode.

commit.message:
Optimize token cache remove path and add filter-first-clone flight for filtered retrieval, Fixes AB#3570409 (#3074)

Fixes
[AB#3570409](https://identitydivision.visualstudio.com/fac9d424-53d2-45c0-91b5-ef6ba7a6bf26/_workitems/edit/3570409)
`removeAccount()`/`removeCredential()` were triggering decrypt-all via
`keySet()` → `getAll()` just to check key existence. Adds a flight-gated
optimization for filtered retrieval and elapsed-time telemetry for
`deleteAccessTokensWithIntersectingScopes`.

## Changes

- **`removeAccount()` / `removeCredential()`**: Replace
`mSharedPreferencesFileManager.keySet().contains(cacheKey)` with O(1)
in-memory `containsKey()` on `mCachedAccountRecordsWithKeys` /
`mCachedCredentialsWithKeys`

- **Flight `ENABLE_FILTER_THEN_CLONE_IN_MEMORY_CACHE`**: When enabled,
`getAccountsFilteredBy()` / `getCredentialsFilteredBy()` filter directly
on uncloned in-memory references, then clone only matching items —
avoiding cloning records that will be filtered out. `getAccounts()` /
`getCredentials()` are unaffected and always return cloned lists.

- **Telemetry**: Add
`elapsed_time_cache_delete_access_tokens_with_intersecting_scopes` span
attribute in `MsalOAuth2TokenCache`; remove unused
`elapsed_time_save_account_shared_preferences`; add
`is_filter_then_clone_enabled` attribute in `getCredentials()`

- **Tests**: `KeySetTrackingStorage` wrapper asserts
`keySet()`/`getAll()` are never called during remove; flight on/off
tests verify clone-only-matching behavior for filtered methods

- **Changelog**: vNext `[PATCH]` entry `#3074`
